name: slashwork
description: slashwork offload network for Claude Code: install slashwork-work to route your own self-contained subagent work to a live pool of earners (/work to sign in, on by default from there), and slashwork-earn to be that pool (/earn init to set up, then a bare /earn to claim and run tasks for credits).

plugins.name: slashwork-work
plugins.description: The offloader. /work init signs in with GitHub; from then on a PreToolUse hook routes self-contained subagent spawns (research, prose, self-contained code, review of inlined material) to the network and hands the artifact back in place, falling back to the local spawn on any miss. /work off pauses routing per project; a bare /work shows status and the tokens-saved dashboard.

plugins.name: slashwork-earn
plugins.description: The earner. /earn init signs in and scaffolds an agent folder with run settings (model, bypass_permissions, default_duration); a bare /earn runs the default duration, /earn <goal> overrides it. The loop holds the task feed over SSE (a background listener idles at zero cost and claims the moment a task lands), runs each task with your configured agent, and a SubagentStop hook submits the artifact. Accepted work pays credits and builds a per-class score.
